pr.title: Match the first function of gobjgxlink
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched the first function in sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink by replacing the assembly implementation of func_8039063C with a C implementation named GObj_GXReorder. The function relinks an HSD_GObj into the GX/render-order linked list using gx_link, prev_gx, next_gx, and the global head/tail arrays lbl_804D7824 and lbl_804D7820. The PR also added a gobjgxlink header, updated build lists to compile the new C object, renamed callsites in remaining assembly from func_8039063C to GObj_GXReorder, and changed HSD_GObj p_link and gx_link fields from signed s8 to unsigned u8 to match observed byte-index behavior.
what_changed: Removed the assembly body for func_8039063C from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c containing void GObj_GXReorder(HSD_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* hiprio_gobj).
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.h with an include guard, gobj.h include, and the GObj_GXReorder prototype.
Updated assembly callsites in gobjgxlink.s to branch to GObj_GXReorder instead of func_8039063C while leaving later functions in assembly.
Updated include/functions.h to replace the unknown func_8039063C entry with GObj_GXReorder.
Added gobjgxlink.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk and added the source object before the remaining asm gobjgxlink.o in obj_files.mk.
Changed HSD_GObj fields p_link and gx_link from s8 to u8 in src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h.
smart_moves: Converted only the matched first function and kept the rest of gobjgxlink.s in assembly, allowing incremental progress without requiring the entire file to be decompiled.
Preserved remaining assembly references by renaming call targets to the new C symbol GObj_GXReorder, which documents the function's role while maintaining linkage.
Used a local u32 link = gobj->gx_link before indexing lbl_804D7824, matching the assembly pattern that loads gx_link as an unsigned byte and shifts/indexes it.
Recognized that gx_link and p_link should be unsigned byte fields, which aligns with lbz usage in the removed assembly and avoids signed-index semantics.

Modeled the function as linked-list insertion after a higher-priority GObj or at the head of the GX-link bucket when no higher-priority object exists, then fixed the successor's prev_gx or the global tail pointer.
decomp_lessons: When assembly uses lbz for a struct field that later indexes an array, represent the field as u8 rather than s8 to preserve unsigned load behavior.
A single matched function inside an otherwise-assembly file can be split into src plus asm by adding both objects to the build in the correct order and removing only the matched assembly symbol.
Meaningful names can often be inferred from data-structure behavior: this function became GObj_GXReorder because it rewires prev_gx/next_gx for GX ordering.

Global arrays with unknown labels can still be used as externs when their behavior is clear enough from indexing and assignment patterns; here lbl_804D7824 acts as a head/high-priority array and lbl_804D7820 as a tail/low-priority array for gx_link buckets.
For linked-list routines, directly mapping assembly stores to struct fields can expose both field signedness and list invariants.
naming_conventions: Matched baselib GObj functions use a GObj_ prefix plus a descriptive action name, as in GObj_GXReorder.
The header include guard used lowercase underscored style: _gobjgxlink_h_.
The decompiled source/header path mirrors the assembly module path: sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.
Temporary or still-unknown globals retained lbl_804D7820 and lbl_804D7824 names rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The removed assembly begins by lbz-loading gobj->gx_link at offset 3, storing hiprio_gobj to gobj->prev_gx at offset 0x14, then conditionally using either hiprio_gobj->next_gx or lbl_804D7824[gx_link] as gobj->next_gx.
The C implementation mirrors the branch structure: if hiprio_gobj is non-NULL, insert after it; otherwise insert at the head array for the object's gx_link.
The final conditional mirrors the assembly tail update: if gobj->next_gx exists, set its prev_gx to gobj; otherwise store gobj into lbl_804D7820[gobj->gx_link].
Remaining assembly branches were retargeted from func_8039063C to GObj_GXReorder, preserving callsite behavior while improving symbol readability.
Build files were adjusted so the new C object contributes the matched symbol while the residual assembly object still supplies the unmatched functions.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename lbl_804D7820 or lbl_804D7824 to semantic GX-link head/tail array names.
Find subsequent gobjgxlink matches for func_8039069C, func_8039075C, func_803907C8, and func_803909D8.
Check whether the _gobjgxlink_h_ include guard style was later normalized to project header conventions.
Search for other HSD_GObj field signedness fixes driven by lbz versus lbz/stb assembly evidence.
